title: Can You? Yes, I Can
description: CAN for ability (I can swim) and requests (Can you help me?). Short answers: Yes, I can / No, I can't.
summary_pt_br: CAN para habilidade e pedidos. Estrutura: CAN + verbo base. Pergunta: Can you swim? Resposta curta: Yes I can / No I can't. Pedidos educados: 'Can you help me?', 'Can I have...?'

scenes.text_en:
'Can you SURF?' Bia asked Joca.
'I can't surf,' he said. 'But I CAN CODE. Can you code?'
'I can't code. But I CAN COOK rice.' Rafa grinned: 'I CAN do both.'
scenes.text_pt:
'Você sabe SURFAR?' Bia perguntou pro Joca.
'Não sei surfar,' ele disse. 'Mas sei PROGRAMAR. Você programa?'
'Eu não sei. Mas sei COZINHAR arroz.' Rafa riu: 'Eu sei os dois.'

scenes.title: CAN + base verb
scenes.body_en: CAN is a modal — never changes form, never takes TO. 'I can swim.' NOT 'I can to swim' or 'I cans swim'. Negative: CAN'T (or CANNOT). Short answer: Yes, I can / No, I can't.
scenes.body_pt: CAN é modal — não muda e nunca vai com TO. 'I can swim' (não 'I can to swim'). Negativa: can't / cannot. Resposta curta: Yes I can / No I can't.

examples.en: Can you help me? → Yes, I can. / No, I can't.

exercise.question: Which is correct?
exercise.explanation: CAN + BASE verb. No 'to', no -ing, no -s.

exercise.question: Complete: '___ you help me with this?'
Answer: 'Can you dance?' — No.
exercise.explanation: Short: 'No, I can't.' Or full: 'No, I cannot dance.'
exercise.hint_pt_br: Short: 'No, I can't'. Full: 'No, I cannot dance'.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Can You? Yes, I Can". CAN for ability (I can swim) and requests (Can you help me?). Short answers: Yes, I can / No, I can't. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Can You? Yes, I Can". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Can You? Yes, I Can'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Can You? Yes, I Can".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
